text.title: WireGuard VPN: Concepts
text.summary: WireGuard uses cryptokey routing: each peer's AllowedIPs list defines which source IPs its key is trusted to send from, and which destination IPs get encrypted to that peer. ~4,000 lines of kernel code vs OpenVPN's 100,000+.

section.title: Cryptokey Routing
section.p: When a packet arrives on a WireGuard interface, the source IP is checked against every peer's AllowedIPs. If it matches, the packet is accepted and decrypted with that peer's key. If it does not match any peer's AllowedIPs, the packet is silently dropped.

rule.statement: AllowedIPs serves two purposes: ingress filter (which source IPs to accept) and egress routing table (which destination IPs to send to this peer).
rule.rationale: This dual role is the core of WireGuard's simplicity — no separate routing table entries needed, no certificate authority, just keys and IP ranges.

example.code:
# Server sees packets from 10.0.0.2 → trusts them if signed by laptop's key # Server sees packets for 10.0.0.2 → encrypts and sends to laptop
Peer (laptop): AllowedIPs = 10.0.0.2/32 Peer (RPi + LAN): AllowedIPs = 10.0.0.3/32, 192.168.1.0/24

list.item: Private key — generated with `wg genkey`. Never leave the device. chmod 600.
Public key — derived from private key with `wg pubkey`. Share with all peers who need to connect to you.
Preshared key — optional symmetric key shared between two specific peers. Adds a post-quantum resistance layer. Generated with `wg genpsk`.

section.title: Network Architecture
section.p: VPS acts as the hub. All peers connect to it. Peer-to-peer traffic routes through the VPS unless direct routing is configured.

example.code: VPS wg0: 10.0.0.1/24 (hub) Peer: dev laptop 10.0.0.2/32 (split tunnel) Peer: home RPi 10.0.0.3/32 + 192.168.1.0/24 (site-to-site) Peer: phone 10.0.0.4/32 (full tunnel)

section.title: Full Tunnel vs Split Tunnel
section.p: The only difference is AllowedIPs on the client config.

list.item: Full tunnel: `AllowedIPs = 0.0.0.0/0, ::/0` — all traffic routes through VPN
Split tunnel: `AllowedIPs = 10.0.0.0/24` — only VPN subnet uses tunnel, internet goes direct

see-also.link: Server config with multiple peer types
